content: For Stripe subscriptions, MDI cases, and Persona KYC verifications, our DB holds a *mirror* updated via webhooks — never the source of truth. Three rules: (1) every mirror table has `last_synced_at` + `<provider>_updated_at`; (2) webhook handlers drop stale events (compare timestamps); (3) a periodic reconciler pulls from the vendor and corrects drift. Skipping the reconciler is what bites — webhooks drop more often than expected.
context: Pattern emerged from the Apoth architecture plan (2026-05-19) covering Stripe, MDI, and Persona. Codified across T-026 (Stripe webhook), T-057 (MDI webhook), T-050 (Persona webhook), T-064 (MDI reconciler), T-069 (Stripe ↔ MDI reconciler). The "no PHI in our DB" decision makes this even more important — we can't even reconstruct state from local data; the vendor's truth must be reconciled.
title: Mirror integrations; never make local state the source of truth
